name: db_only_phase_wave1_trigger_and_event_truth
outcome_goal: Workflow trigger and event truth are driven by DB change events, durable checkpointing, and one explicit notification-consumption model.
anti_requirements: Do not keep LISTEN registered without consuming notifications.
Do not keep the legacy processed-flag seam as the canonical checkpoint model.
Do not widen this wave into unrelated mutation or command-bus work.

jobs.label: db_change_trigger_expansion
jobs.prompt: Implement `BUG-661DC83D` by attaching the existing DB-change event trigger function to the workflow and receipt tables that should actually emit system events. Keep the migration idempotent, avoid event storms on tables that only need INSERT coverage, and do not rewrite the trigger function itself.

jobs.label: notify_consumption_cutover
jobs.prompt: Resolve `BUG-0388B701` by choosing one explicit event-consumption model. Either implement real LISTEN/NOTIFY consumption with a durable listener, or demote LISTEN and make polling the declared authority. Remove the current half-wired TODO state so the runtime is honest about how events are consumed.

jobs.label: processed_flag_seam_delete
jobs.prompt: Delete the legacy processed-flag seam behind `BUG-14B00013` so trigger consumption advances through durable subscription checkpoints instead of the old `system_events.processed` truth model. Treat checkpoint-backed subscription state as the only supported contract; do not preserve or reintroduce a processed-flag fallback.

jobs.label: trigger_replay_regression
jobs.prompt: Add or tighten regression coverage proving that DB-trigger expansion, notification truth, and durable subscription checkpoints still replay correctly after the Wave 1 cutover. The proof must exercise checkpoint advancement and confirm we do not silently double-process old events.
